primary_changes: Added PlanFeature junction model (many-to-many relationship between Plan and Feature)
Added OrganizationUsage model for tracking resource consumption per tenant
Updated module docstring to reflect newly added models

PlanFeature.purpose: Junction table linking Plans to Features with plan-specific configuration

OrganizationUsage.purpose: Tracks resource usage per organization for metering and quota enforcement

patterns_discovered.pattern: Consistent use of UUID primary keys
patterns_discovered.evidence: Both PlanFeature and OrganizationUsage use uuid.uuid4 as default for id field
patterns_discovered.significance: Aligns with platform architecture for distributed systems

patterns_discovered.evidence: PlanFeature.value and OrganizationUsage.metadata use JSONField with dict defaults
patterns_discovered.significance: Enables extensible feature configuration without schema migrations

patterns_discovered.pattern: Helper methods for type-specific data access
patterns_discovered.evidence: PlanFeature has get_limit(), get_credits(), get_reset_period(); OrganizationUsage has remaining_quota and usage_percentage properties
patterns_discovered.significance: Encapsulates business logic for feature value retrieval, reducing client-side complexity

patterns_discovered.evidence: Both models include detailed docstrings with practical code examples
patterns_discovered.significance: Facilitates developer onboarding and reduces integration errors

patterns_discovered.pattern: Soft delete capability through mixin inheritance
patterns_discovered.evidence: Both models inherit TimeStampedMixin; PlanFeature also includes SoftDeleteMixin
patterns_discovered.significance: Maintains audit trail while allowing logical deletion

patterns_discovered.evidence: Indexes on foreign keys, frequently filtered combinations, and sort fields
patterns_discovered.significance: Optimizes common query patterns (filter by plan+enabled, organization+period_end)

patterns_discovered.evidence: is_boolean, is_limit, is_usage properties in PlanFeature; is_unlimited, is_limit_exceeded in OrganizationUsage
patterns_discovered.significance: Provides clear business logic semantics at model level

gotchas_discovered.description: The diff was truncated at 19119 characters, cutting off the end of OrganizationUsage model implementation
gotchas_discovered.impact: Cannot verify complete implementation of all methods in OrganizationUsage (at least 2-3 methods likely missing from view)
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Review full file directly to ensure all increment(), reset(), and period management methods are properly implemented

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Feature type checking via FeatureType enum
gotchas_discovered.description: PlanFeature properties (is_boolean, is_limit, is_usage) reference FeatureType enum that must be defined in Feature model
gotchas_discovered.impact: Will raise AttributeError at runtime if FeatureType not properly imported or defined
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Verify FeatureType enum is imported and Feature model is complete

gotchas_discovered.description: OrganizationUsage uses -1 to represent unlimited quota (not null or special constant)
gotchas_discovered.impact: Requires discipline in checks (is_unlimited property exists, but raw queries must account for -1)
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Always use is_unlimited property; avoid direct usage_limit comparisons

gotchas_discovered.description: OrganizationUsage assumes periodic reset cycles but implementation details for reset() method are cut off
gotchas_discovered.impact: Unclear how period_start/period_end are managed or auto-rolled over
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Implement or verify scheduled task for resetting periods; consider timezone handling

gotchas_discovered.description: OrganizationUsage enforces one usage record per org-feature pair
gotchas_discovered.impact: If same feature needs multiple usage tracks (e.g., daily + monthly), this design won't support it
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Consider if feature-scoped period tracking is sufficient; may need period identifier in unique constraint

approach_outcome.summary: Successfully created two new Django models (PlanFeature and OrganizationUsage) that enable plan-feature relationships and resource usage tracking
key_accomplishments: PlanFeature junction model with plan-specific configuration override capability
OrganizationUsage model with comprehensive quota enforcement properties
Rich helper methods and properties for common feature/usage operations
Proper indexing strategy for query performance

implementation_quality.code_organization: Well-structured with clear separation of concerns
implementation_quality.documentation: Excellent - includes class docstrings, field help_text, and usage examples
implementation_quality.performance_considerations: Good - strategic indexes on common filter combinations
implementation_quality.extensibility: High - JSONField-based configuration allows future schema extensions

approach_outcome.completeness_assessment: Appears 95% complete; missing only the end of OrganizationUsage due to diff truncation

recommendations.recommendation: Verify the complete OrganizationUsage model by reading the full file to ensure all methods (increment, reset, period management) are properly implemented
Create integration tests verifying: PlanFeature cascade behavior on Plan/Feature deletion, OrganizationUsage quota enforcement, period reset logic
Ensure FeatureType enum and Feature model are complete and exported; add explicit imports at module top if needed
Create database views or raw SQL helpers for common queries (e.g., all enabled features for plan, usage summary per org)
Implement Celery task for OrganizationUsage period resets (handle multiple reset frequencies: daily, monthly, etc.)
Create Django admin inlines for PlanFeature (nested within Plan admin) for convenient feature management
Add timezone-aware comparison in is_period_active property; use timezone.now() consistently throughout usage tracking
Add migration dependencies documentation; consider adding state transition diagram for period lifecycle in OrganizationUsage
